meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Students: Master SaaS Metrics
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap from student to SaaS builder. Master professional analytics setup using Antigravity and Claude Code in this advanced-level engineering guide.
content.answer_block: Transform your student project into a professional SaaS by implementing event-driven analytics. By integrating Antigravity for observability and Claude Code for automated instrumentation, you capture user intent data immediately, enabling data-driven feature iteration and proving your engineering rigor to future employers and potential investors.
content.intro:
You have the code, you have the vision, but you are flying blind. Most student developers treat their applications like black boxes, unaware of how users actually interact with their features. This 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier is the single greatest obstacle between a hobbyist project and a professional-grade portfolio piece.

Today, we stop guessing. We are moving beyond simple console logs to professional-grade telemetry. By leveraging Antigravity for real-time observability and Claude Code for rapid instrumentation, you will gain the technical maturity to track user journeys, identify drop-off points, and demonstrate a level of product-sense that separates senior engineers from the rest of the pack.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that define 'user success' in your app. This ensures your telemetry captures intent, not just noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Engineer. Review my app's user flow and suggest 5 high-signal event triggers for tracking onboarding friction and feature activation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze my current codebase and list the top 5 user interaction points that require event logging.'

steps.title: Automated Instrumentation Setup
steps.description: Inject tracking logic into your codebase without manual boilerplate. Use Claude Code to wrap your critical functions with analytics middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable analytics decorator for my API endpoints that captures user ID, event name, and timestamp, forwarding it to the Antigravity collector.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the new analytics decorator to all functions in the /controllers directory.'

steps.title: Configuring the Antigravity Collector
steps.description: Set up the ingestion pipeline to ensure data integrity. Proper configuration prevents duplicate event firing and ensures low-latency reporting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the Antigravity collector SDK with session batching and privacy-first filtering to handle high-concurrency event streams.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the Antigravity SDK and create a configuration file for event batching and error handling.'

steps.title: Validating the Data Pipeline
steps.description: Verify your instrumentation is working by simulating user behavior. Never ship analytics without a local integration test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that simulates a user sign-up flow and verifies that the Antigravity collector receives the expected event payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute a local test script to validate that analytics events are firing correctly on button clicks.'

steps.title: Building the Analytics Dashboard
steps.description: Visualize your data to make decisions. Turn raw logs into actionable insights that demonstrate your project's impact.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a SQL query to extract daily active users and feature adoption rates from my Antigravity event logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Export the current analytics schema and create a dashboard configuration file for the primary metrics.'

pro_tips: Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information); always hash user IDs before sending them to third-party analytics tools.
Implement 'sampling' for high-volume events to reduce infrastructure costs while maintaining statistical significance.
Use custom event properties to track feature versioning, allowing you to see if new updates improve engagement.
Set up automated alerts in Antigravity for anomalous drops in traffic, which often signal deployment bugs.
Treat your analytics schema like your database schema; document it and keep it version-controlled in your repo.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://analytics-handbook.dev
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior. Use the data to identify the one feature most users abandon, then use Claude Code to refactor that specific flow based on the evidence.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an engineer who builds with intent. Don't let your hard work go unmeasured. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
